element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic for overworld navigation and enemy encounters
Progressive weapon upgrades found by visiting family members across seven houses plus mansion
Transition from overhead to first-person style 3D hallway traversal for boss encounters

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Five buildings throughout city (excluding optional first building)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each boss defeat grants puzzle piece and refills all items; no backtracking allowed after defeat
Required to access otherwise inaccessible aboveground areas; second main overworld zone

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Accessed after all five bosses defeated
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Alien ship computer boss; final challenge to prevent invasion
Summons Lurch to destroy all enemies on screen; utility item for crowd control
Collected from each of five bosses; likely required for progression or story completion

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating a boss (refill all items and supplies)
Obtaining new weapon from family member
Returning to safe overworld areas from building interiors
Using Noose item to clear screen
typical_tension_triggers: Entering 3D building interior (perspective shift and confinement)
No backtracking allowed after boss defeat (forced forward progression)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cycle of overworld exploration with constant minor threats, punctuated by building dungeons with escalating boss difficulty; unbalanced difficulty noted with easy mini-quests but next-to-impossible bosses

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Seven houses plus Addams mansion accessible via secret path through trees; optional first building skips its unused boss
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden path through trees behind mansion provides secret access to Gomez and family areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Very easy mini-quests with minimal challenge during house visits and weapon collection
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing enemy density in overworld areas (streets, sewers); building interiors become more complex
difficulty_curve.late_game: Extreme spike with next-to-impossible bosses; final UFO boss requires mastery of all mechanics
notable_spikes: Boss encounters described as 'next to impossible' by reviewers despite easy preceding content
5th boss features multi-directional head similar to unused four-headed enemy (suggests complexity escalation)
Final alien ship computer boss with animated eye mechanics and turrets

notable_level_patterns.description: Three overworld areas (streets, sewers, UFO platform) with constantly respawning enemies; primary navigation view; allows access to buildings and family member encounters
Transform from overhead to 3D first-person hallway crawl perspective; six buildings total with hallway traversal akin to dungeon crawl; five contain boss encounters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: First building (optional, no boss) through fifth building (mandatory boss)

notable_level_patterns.description: Seven encounters with Addams Family members (Thing appears three times, plus Wednesday, Gomez, Morticia, Grandmama, Pugsley) distributed throughout buildings; provide weapons and items; optional Gomez encounter via secret path
Climactic room in five buildings; defeats grant puzzle piece, UFO picture, and complete item refill; no re-entry allowed after defeat (one-way progression)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Building interiors containing enormous alien bosses

notable_level_patterns.description: UFO platform accessed only after all five puzzle pieces collected; features final alien ship computer boss; culminates invasion prevention

sources_used: Wikipedia - Fester's Quest (Gameplay section, Story section, Reception)
The Cutting Room Floor - Fester's Quest (Unused Graphics and Bosses sections, Regional Differences)
